title: Chocolate decadence – it falls where it wants

seo.title: Chocolate decadence - it falls where it wants - Marinda Kook

html:
Sometimes you just feel like making something so decadent that when you eat it, you get goosebumps on your cheeks from the deliciousness. This tart is that. It's easy and quick. It's always there for the day when an unexpected man comes over the hill with ‘n sweet tooth on his body. Just 4 ingredients ( the 5th is optional)

NEEDED

  1. 1 packet Tennis biscuits ( you can use ginger biscuits or chocolate biscuits too) I used chocolate Tennis biscuits.
  2. 125ml butter
  3. 4 x 180g whole-nut chocolate slabs ( if I can't get Lindt I use Cadbury)
  4. 250 ml cream
  5. 1 x Flake for decoration ( or cherries or just whatever you feel like)

NOW:

  • chop the biscuits finely in the food processor or roll them fine in a plastic bag with a rolling pin
  • melt the butter and mix it with the crushed biscuits. Press the mixture firmly into a tart dish
  • break the chocolate into a bowl that can go into the microwave and melt it on full power for 2 minutes
  • add the cream and microwave for another 1 minute on full power. Stir the mixture until it is well combined
  • pour it onto your biscuit base and sprinkle the Flake over. Cover with clingwrap and put it in the freezer (yes freezer and not fridge)

You always have something sweet in your freezer for the unexpected visitor. Take it out and cut it with a warm knife. If I know I'm going to use it for dessert, I take it out about an hour ahead of time to soften a little. Cut thin slices, because it's very rich.
